452fcabc-5f12-4792-8254-847cf60c12e3.name: Vanilla Pumpkin Protein Cheesecake
452fcabc-5f12-4792-8254-847cf60c12e3.description: Base protein cheesecake (vanilla SF pudding, pumpkin for moisture/body). Launch point for flavor variants. Low-fat / high-protein; pumpkin does the work fat does in a standard cheesecake. Tuned DENSE (Tier 1+2): pumpkin reduced 120->75g + 2 tbsp AP flour for a firmer set (AP because that's what's on hand — its gluten sets denser than cornstarch), baked DRY (no water bath) to a just-set center and chilled overnight — denser at near-zero macro cost (~129 cal/slice, ratio 9.1). Stays lean; if a bake comes out rubbery rather than densely creamy, add a little fat (more cream cheese or an extra yolk).
452fcabc-5f12-4792-8254-847cf60c12e3.author: user

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks, and we want a dense set anyway).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

02d128b1-b3a9-4889-aa55-c5c0db865946.name: Crumbl Base Protein Cookie
02d128b1-b3a9-4889-aa55-c5c0db865946.description: The shared base cookie for the crumbl protein-cookie family — the flavor-neutral vanilla chassis every flavor derives from. Built from the tested-excellent set: Birthday Cake + Lemon dough at 1.5 scoops plus their shared cornstarch soft-crumb trick, with a lean egg white for structure. Frosting is a sub_recipe pin (defaults to the Cool-Whip vanilla protein frosting); flavors swap the pin — cream cheese for red velvet, a lemon drizzle for lemon. The 20g newcomer's heavier protein-load stays a queued experiment. Sweetener is granulated Splenda (calorically modeled, never monk fruit).
02d128b1-b3a9-4889-aa55-c5c0db865946.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients into a bowl — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder, salt.
Add the wet ingredients — egg, melted butter, Greek yogurt, almond milk, vanilla — and mix to a dough. If dry, add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time until it just comes together.
Chill 20-30 min, then scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, and flatten (this dough barely spreads).
Bake at 350F for ~9 minutes — pull while the centers still look soft; they firm up as they cool.
Cool the cookies fully, then frost with the vanilla protein frosting (sub-recipe).

unresolved: vanilla protein frosting: sub-recipe 1074a98e-b1c9-44ac-af4b-d4a3115c8768 not loaded

ff4e23b8-cd4a-4e66-83ec-5394ad7f2ab9.name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies
ff4e23b8-cd4a-4e66-83ec-5394ad7f2ab9.description: Browned-butter protein chocolate-chip cookies, makes 3. VERSION A - Thick & Gooey (molten center): tall bakery-style mounds, intentionally uneven (gooey center, firmer edges). The thick middle cooks slowest, so the edges finish before the center - that's the price of a molten center. Soft, fragile, and pale is CORRECT; these don't brown like a normal cookie. Confirm doneness by breaking one open after resting: soft/dense/moist/baked = done; wet/gummy/raw-flour = 1-2 min more. (Version B - Even & Soft-Chewy - is a derived variant that flattens + bakes lower for one consistent texture.)
ff4e23b8-cd4a-4e66-83ec-5394ad7f2ab9.author: user

steps.instructionText: Brown the butter: melt it in a pan over medium heat, swirling, until golden and nutty-smelling (3-4 min). Pour into a mixing bowl and let cool a couple minutes so it won't cook the egg.
Mix wet: whisk in the brown sugar, Splenda, whole egg, egg white, and vanilla until smooth and glossy. Don't whip air in - just combine.

Add dry: stir in the whey, flour, pudding mix, baking soda, and salt until a thick, scoopable dough forms, then fold in the chocolate chips. It should hold its shape, not pour. Too stiff/dry -> a splash of milk; too loose -> a bit more flour. Taste for sweetness.
Chill the dough 30 minutes.
Shape into 3 tall mounds - do NOT flatten.
Bake at 350F / 175C for 9-11 minutes. Pull when the edges are set but the center still looks slightly underdone/soft. Stays pale - don't wait for golden tops.
Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.

8a1977d5-b587-49f1-b896-7d6d3f69ba16.name: Protein Cream-Cheese Frosting
8a1977d5-b587-49f1-b896-7d6d3f69ba16.description: Protein cream-cheese frosting — extracted from Red Velvet Protein Cookies as an M3 sub-recipe. Makes 1 batch (enough to frost one 5-cookie set). Its own base→variant root: Cool-Whip and other frostings derive from this.
8a1977d5-b587-49f1-b896-7d6d3f69ba16.author: user

steps.instructionText: Beat the yogurt, cream cheese, protein, and Splenda smooth. Add the pudding mix to thicken, or chill 20-30 min before using.

270e2a77-d446-408e-935c-690d6cb1b6f0.name: Red Velvet Protein Cookies (Oat Base)
270e2a77-d446-408e-935c-690d6cb1b6f0.description: Lean oat-flour red velvet with a protein cream-cheese frosting (frosting is a sub_recipe pin). The oat-base branch of the red velvet bake-off, rehabbed from the original floppy version for structure: pumpkin cut 0.5 -> 0.25 cup, oat flour doubled 0.25 -> 0.5 cup, cornstarch dropped, baking soda halved, an egg white added for set, and baked lower-and-longer (325F, 15-18 min). Yogurt kept for tang and protein. Sibling branch is the Crumbl-base version; bake both and promote the winner. Standalone root. Untested at this formulation.
270e2a77-d446-408e-935c-690d6cb1b6f0.author: user

steps.instructionText: Preheat oven to 325F. Line a tray with parchment.
Whisk the wets - yogurt, pumpkin, egg, egg white, vanilla, food coloring, vinegar - then stir in the Splenda.
Add the dries - cocoa, oat flour, pudding, protein, baking soda, salt. Mix to a thick dough.
Rest 10-15 min to hydrate. If still batter-like, add oat flour 1 tbsp at a time until scoopable.
Scoop 5 cookies and shape into pucks (they barely spread). Bake 15-18 min at 325F until the tops spring back to a light touch and the edges are set.
Cool fully on the tray - they firm up as they cool.
Beat the yogurt, cream cheese, protein, and Splenda smooth. Add pudding mix to thicken, or chill 20-30 min. Frost the cooled cookies.

e888cb1b-72b4-43e8-ab27-464b0b62baa4.name: High-Protein Anti-Pancake Sugar Cookies
e888cb1b-72b4-43e8-ab27-464b0b62baa4.description: Low-flour protein sugar cookie tuned to kill the rubbery 'pancake' texture: a scoop of whey replaces most flour and SF instant pudding binds moisture for a dense bakery chew. Sweetener is granulated Splenda; pudding modeled as the Simply Delish SF vanilla already in the library; butter is regular unsalted, already halved to 7 g. Leavening is baking powder only (no soda / cream of tartar). Final step is a fridge rest. Source: user. Stated batch macros ~428 kcal / 44P / 41C / 11F (107/cookie at 4).
e888cb1b-72b4-43e8-ab27-464b0b62baa4.author: user

steps.instructionText: Whisk the dry base — AP flour, whey protein, SF pudding mix, Splenda, baking powder, salt — until there are no protein clumps.
Add the melted butter, whole egg, Greek yogurt, and vanilla into the dry mix.
Stir to combine, then stop and let the bowl sit untouched 5 minutes so the whey and pudding starches swell into a shapeable dough. If still tacky, chill 5 more minutes.
Preheat oven/air fryer to 325F. Divide into 4 equal balls on parchment and press down firmly with your palm or a glass — this dough sets in the shape you give it, it won't spread.
Bake at 325F for 6-7 minutes. Pull the moment the edges look dry and set, even if the centers still look slightly soft — whey dries out fast.
Leave the cookies on the hot sheet 5-10 minutes; residual pan heat finishes the centers without making the protein rubbery.
Final step: rest in the fridge ~10 minutes to fully set, then eat.

d6efef0d-eabe-449b-991c-685f68348f95.name: High-Protein Nanaimo Bars
d6efef0d-eabe-449b-991c-685f68348f95.description: A high-protein, lower-calorie take on the classic three-layer Canadian Nanaimo bar: cocoa-coconut base, butterscotch protein filling, baking-chocolate top. Sweetener modeled as granulated Splenda; the 'or 20g oat flour' alt for coconut flour is not taken. Source: instagram. Stated full batch ~1300 kcal / 83C / 118P / 58F (header lists per-bar at 10 bars, but the directions slice 9 — yield set to 9).
d6efef0d-eabe-449b-991c-685f68348f95.author: user

steps.instructionText: Toast the shredded coconut ~30 seconds in a nonstick pan until golden brown.
Mix the dry base ingredients (with the toasted coconut), then add the almond milk and egg whites.
Line a baking dish with parchment and press down the base layer.
Mix the filling ingredients to a glossy frosting consistency and spread over the base.
Freeze 20 minutes.
Melt the baking chocolate and coconut oil together in 30-second intervals; sweeten the melt with liquid monk fruit to taste.
Pour and spread the chocolate over the bars.
Set in the fridge ~20 min uncovered, then refrigerate overnight covered. Slice into 9 bars.

5776f7b3-647f-4ca8-a52a-a9d0d632c13e.name: Protein Ice Cream Sandwich
5776f7b3-647f-4ca8-a52a-a9d0d632c13e.description: Healthy protein ice cream sandwiches in the style of Fat Boys: a blended vanilla protein 'ice cream' between chocolate graham crackers. Source: healthbeet.org. Decent as-is; the filling really wants a Ninja Creami churn for texture. Stated macros ~135 kcal / 16C / 13-14P / 3F per sandwich.
5776f7b3-647f-4ca8-a52a-a9d0d632c13e.author: user

steps.instructionText: Blend the protein powder, almond milk, pudding mix, Greek yogurt, vanilla, and salt smooth in a blender.
Freeze ~6 hours, until mostly frozen. Re-churn in a Ninja Creami (best) or re-blend in a food processor.
Line a cake pan with parchment and arrange half-sheets of graham cracker, 6 across the bottom.
Spread the ice cream over the crackers until all are covered.
Top with a second graham cracker and return to the freezer until frozen. Slice into 6 sandwiches.

c2de078f-82fc-4ad8-bf8c-30fc322cb675.name: 20g Protein Frosted Cookies
c2de078f-82fc-4ad8-bf8c-30fc322cb675.description: Macro-friendly frosted protein cookie (Instagram) ingested as a crumbl base-chassis candidate. Notable for a claimed <10 kcal per g protein, achieved by protein-loading: 3 scoops whey in only 5 cookies plus a full cup of nonfat Greek yogurt in the frosting. Modeled with regular AP flour (source said 'Bob's Red Mill GF flour or regular flour'); generic sweetener -> granulated Splenda; the 1 egg white authored as 33 g (one large white) since the library egg-white has no count equivalence; sprinkles ('to taste') omitted so it reads as a clean unfrosted-chassis sibling to the other crumbl cookies. Component keys aligned with the Birthday Cake base so compare row-aligns and promote can graft by key. Not yet made.
c2de078f-82fc-4ad8-bf8c-30fc322cb675.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients together — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder.
Add the wet ingredients (egg, egg white, melted butter, almond milk, vanilla) to the dry and mix to a dough.
Coat your hands in flour to make the dough easier to shape; divide into 5 cookies on a lined sheet.
Bake at 350F for 10 minutes.
Allow the cookies to cool fully before frosting.
Mix the Greek yogurt, SF vanilla pudding mix, vanilla, and a dash of salt into a frosting; spread on the cooled cookies (add sprinkles to taste).

1b40ba90-2501-4707-925c-ab0c9acc93cc.name: Giant Single-Serve Protein Cookie
1b40ba90-2501-4707-925c-ab0c9acc93cc.description: Yogurt-based high-protein cookie (no butter/sugar) found on Instagram, untried. Coconut flour subbed to oat flour; sugar-free chips to semi-sweet; 'zero cal sugar' to granulated Splenda. Chocolate chips unspecified in source — assumed 20g.
1b40ba90-2501-4707-925c-ab0c9acc93cc.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix all the ingredients together until smooth, folding in the chocolate chips last.
Divide onto parchment paper and press each into a flat round so it bakes evenly.
Bake 7-9 minutes until the edges are set but the center is still soft.
Let cool and enjoy.

19ae402e-f81b-4e31-a7e4-cd6d43bb4db8.name: Protein Graham Crust
19ae402e-f81b-4e31-a7e4-cd6d43bb4db8.description: Reusable cheesecake crust — graham crumbs bound with egg white + a little melted butter, lightly sweetened, par-baked. Egg-white bound so it sets firm at minimal fat (the binding that tested well on the turtle). This is the base crust a cheesecake composes in; skip it for a crustless cheesecake, or fork it for a flavored version. Sub-recipe (1 batch ~= 305 cal, presses a 9-inch bottom).
19ae402e-f81b-4e31-a7e4-cd6d43bb4db8.author: user

steps.instructionText: Stir the graham crumbs, Splenda, and salt together. Work in the melted butter and egg white until it clumps like wet sand, then press firmly into the bottom of a parchment-lined 9-inch pan — run the parchment up two opposite sides as a sling so you can lift the whole cake out later. Bake at 350F for ~10 min, then cool while you finish the filling.

332eba67-124d-4937-9bb2-11ffeb003230.name: Banana Butterscotch Cinnamon Cheesecake
332eba67-124d-4937-9bb2-11ffeb003230.author: user
332eba67-124d-4937-9bb2-11ffeb003230.commitMessage: derive variant from Vanilla Pumpkin Protein Cheesecake

payload.instructionText: Caramelize the banana: slice and cook in a nonstick pan over medium heat with a sprinkle of sweetener until golden, ~2-3 min per side. Add the pumpkin to the same pan and cook 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. In the last ~45-60 sec, stir the ground cinnamon into the hot fruit to toast it — it should turn fragrant and deepen a shade. Toast it in the fruit, not in a dry pan: loose ground cinnamon scorches in seconds, but the fruit's moisture buffers it so it blooms evenly. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).

Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

payload.instructionText: Caramelize the banana: slice and cook in a nonstick pan over medium heat with a sprinkle of sweetener until golden, ~2-3 min per side. Add the pumpkin to the same pan and cook 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. In the last ~45-60 sec, stir the ground cinnamon into the hot fruit to toast it — it should turn fragrant and deepen a shade. Toast it in the fruit, not in a dry pan: loose ground cinnamon scorches in seconds, but the fruit's moisture buffers it so it blooms evenly. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).

Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles. For the topping, toast the graham crumbs with the garnish cinnamon in a dry pan over medium heat ~1 min, stirring, until fragrant, then let cool. Scatter over the cheesecake after baking so they stay crisp.

2ec69457-3a2e-4bdf-8a7c-fbc327556a17.name: Crumbl Base Protein Cookie
2ec69457-3a2e-4bdf-8a7c-fbc327556a17.description: The shared base cookie for the crumbl protein-cookie family — the flavor-neutral vanilla chassis every flavor derives from. Built from the tested-excellent set: Birthday Cake + Lemon dough at 1.5 scoops plus their shared cornstarch soft-crumb trick, with a lean egg white for structure. Frosting is a sub_recipe pin (defaults to the Cool-Whip vanilla protein frosting); flavors swap the pin — cream cheese for red velvet, a lemon drizzle for lemon. The 20g newcomer's heavier protein-load stays a queued experiment. Sweetener is granulated Splenda (calorically modeled, never monk fruit).
2ec69457-3a2e-4bdf-8a7c-fbc327556a17.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients into a bowl — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder, salt.
Add the wet ingredients — egg, melted butter, Greek yogurt, almond milk, vanilla — and mix to a dough. If dry, add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time until it just comes together.
Chill 20-30 min, then scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, and flatten (this dough barely spreads).
Bake at 350F for ~9 minutes — pull while the centers still look soft; they firm up as they cool.
Cool the cookies fully, then frost with the vanilla protein frosting (sub-recipe).

0aed0257-382b-44b4-9327-f9b90687b3b4.description: Lemon flavor of the Crumbl base — base chassis + lemon juice in the dough, and the base's frosting sub_recipe pin dropped in favor of a lemon drizzle (lemon juice + granulated Splenda; never monk fruit). Derived off Crumbl Base Protein Cookie. NOTE: the drizzle was rated 'bad' on the original (too weak/thin) and granulated Splenda won't glaze as smoothly as a powdered sweetener — still a redesign candidate. Untested at this base chassis.

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients into a bowl — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder, salt.
Add the wet ingredients — egg, melted butter, Greek yogurt, almond milk, vanilla — and mix to a dough. If dry, add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time until it just comes together.
Chill 20-30 min, then scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, and flatten (this dough barely spreads).
Bake at 350F for ~9 minutes — pull while the centers still look soft; they firm up as they cool.
Cool the cookies fully, then frost with the vanilla protein frosting (sub-recipe).

5668808e-0454-4dd3-adee-d6b3086bd467.description: Red velvet flavor of the Crumbl base - base chassis + cocoa, a little apple cider vinegar for the buttermilk tang, and red gel color; the frosting pin is swapped from the base default (vanilla) to the Protein Cream-Cheese Frosting. The Crumbl-base branch of the red velvet bake-off (sibling is the oat-base version). Leavening left as the base's (baking powder, no soda) so the tested texture is unchanged - the vinegar is for tang, not lift. Cocoa makes the dough drier, so it leans on the base's add-almond-milk-to-loosen step. Derived off Crumbl Base Protein Cookie. Untested at this chassis.

c5c6eb5d-3e56-4916-bcac-bef142a24854.name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies (Soft-Chewy)
c5c6eb5d-3e56-4916-bcac-bef142a24854.author: user
c5c6eb5d-3e56-4916-bcac-bef142a24854.commitMessage: derive variant from Browned-Butter Protein Cookies

steps.instructionText: Brown the butter: melt it in a pan over medium heat, swirling, until golden and nutty-smelling (3-4 min). Pour into a mixing bowl and let cool a couple minutes so it won't cook the egg.
Mix wet: whisk in the brown sugar, Splenda, whole egg, egg white, and vanilla until smooth and glossy. Don't whip air in - just combine.

Add dry: stir in the whey, flour, pudding mix, baking soda, and salt until a thick, scoopable dough forms, then fold in the chocolate chips. It should hold its shape, not pour. Too stiff/dry -> a splash of milk; too loose -> a bit more flour. Taste for sweetness.
Chill the dough 30 minutes.
Shape into 3 tall mounds - do NOT flatten.
Bake at 350F / 175C for 9-11 minutes. Pull when the edges are set but the center still looks slightly underdone/soft. Stays pale - don't wait for golden tops.
Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.

payload.instructionText: Chill only ~15 minutes (or let cold dough sit 10 min at room temp before baking). A fridge-cold center lags the most, so less chilling helps the middle keep pace.

Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.

d743e8eb-1ecf-405d-b315-29baa2fa3200.name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies (60g protein)
d743e8eb-1ecf-405d-b315-29baa2fa3200.author: user
d743e8eb-1ecf-405d-b315-29baa2fa3200.commitMessage: derive variant from Browned-Butter Protein Cookies

steps.instructionText: Brown the butter: melt it in a pan over medium heat, swirling, until golden and nutty-smelling (3-4 min). Pour into a mixing bowl and let cool a couple minutes so it won't cook the egg.
Mix wet: whisk in the brown sugar, Splenda, whole egg, egg white, and vanilla until smooth and glossy. Don't whip air in - just combine.

Add dry: stir in the whey, flour, pudding mix, baking soda, and salt until a thick, scoopable dough forms, then fold in the chocolate chips. It should hold its shape, not pour. Too stiff/dry -> a splash of milk; too loose -> a bit more flour. Taste for sweetness.
Chill the dough 30 minutes.
Shape into 3 tall mounds - do NOT flatten.
Bake at 350F / 175C for 9-11 minutes. Pull when the edges are set but the center still looks slightly underdone/soft. Stays pale - don't wait for golden tops.
Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.

d8e732d6-4869-4d92-8072-d8b65e25e3a2.name: Turtle Chocolate Graham Crust
d8e732d6-4869-4d92-8072-d8b65e25e3a2.author: user
d8e732d6-4869-4d92-8072-d8b65e25e3a2.commitMessage: derive variant from Protein Graham Crust

steps.instructionText: Stir the graham crumbs, Splenda, and salt together. Work in the melted butter and egg white until it clumps like wet sand, then press firmly into the bottom of a parchment-lined 9-inch pan — run the parchment up two opposite sides as a sling so you can lift the whole cake out later. Bake at 350F for ~10 min, then cool while you finish the filling.

e42d17c8-77e4-4e15-b109-a38eb6296e8c.name: Red Velvet Protein Cookies (Oat Base)
e42d17c8-77e4-4e15-b109-a38eb6296e8c.description: Lean oat-flour red velvet with a protein cream-cheese frosting (frosting is a sub_recipe pin). The oat-base branch of the red velvet bake-off, rehabbed from the original floppy version for structure: pumpkin cut 0.5 -> 0.25 cup, oat flour doubled 0.25 -> 0.5 cup, cornstarch dropped, baking soda halved, an egg white added for set, and baked lower-and-longer (325F, 15-18 min). Yogurt kept for tang and protein. Sibling branch is the Crumbl-base version; bake both and promote the winner. Standalone root. Untested at this formulation.
e42d17c8-77e4-4e15-b109-a38eb6296e8c.author: user

steps.instructionText: Preheat oven to 325F. Line a tray with parchment.
Whisk the wets - yogurt, pumpkin, egg, egg white, vanilla, food coloring, vinegar - then stir in the Splenda.
Add the dries - cocoa, oat flour, pudding, protein, baking soda, salt. Mix to a thick dough.
Rest 10-15 min to hydrate. If still batter-like, add oat flour 1 tbsp at a time until scoopable.
Scoop 5 cookies and shape into pucks (they barely spread). Bake 15-18 min at 325F until the tops spring back to a light touch and the edges are set.
Cool fully on the tray - they firm up as they cool.
Beat the yogurt, cream cheese, protein, and Splenda smooth. Add pudding mix to thicken, or chill 20-30 min. Frost the cooled cookies.

62b72353-e3a1-4fa6-9b45-82d5a8ebade0.name: Turtle Protein Caramel Swirl
62b72353-e3a1-4fa6-9b45-82d5a8ebade0.description: Protein caramel for marbling into the turtle cheesecake filling — 0% Greek yogurt + butterscotch SF pudding (the caramel note) + granulated Splenda, salted. Thick and spoonable; it lives hidden in the swirl, so it carries protein, not gloss. Sub-recipe (1 batch ~= 99 cal / 12.6 P).
62b72353-e3a1-4fa6-9b45-82d5a8ebade0.author: user

steps.instructionText: Whisk the Greek yogurt, butterscotch pudding mix, Splenda, almond milk, and salt smooth. It should be thick and spoonable like a loose caramel — chill 10 min if you want it firmer to swirl.

37c72d67-02bf-40e9-91b3-a732ab422624.name: Turtle Real Brown-Sugar Caramel
37c72d67-02bf-40e9-91b3-a732ab422624.description: The real, glossy caramel for the top — brown sugar cooked with a little butter and thinned with almond milk, salted. Genuine caramelized-sugar flavor and drizzle (you can't caramelize Splenda), kept deliberately small: ~121 cal for the whole cake, the one honest-sugar indulgence. Sub-recipe (1 batch).
37c72d67-02bf-40e9-91b3-a732ab422624.author: user

steps.instructionText: Melt the butter in a small saucepan, add the brown sugar and salt, and cook over medium, stirring, until it bubbles and darkens slightly (~2-3 min). Off heat, whisk in the almond milk (it will sputter) until smooth. Cool slightly — it thickens as it sits; rewarm to drizzle.

a69655a4-d4fe-4e37-a6e5-4f73a4cf6987.name: Turtle Dark-Chocolate Ganache
a69655a4-d4fe-4e37-a6e5-4f73a4cf6987.description: Chocolate drizzle for the top — unsweetened baking chocolate melted with granulated Splenda and thinned with warm almond milk. Real cocoa-butter mouthfeel that cocoa powder can't fake; kept to a thin drizzle so the fat stays small (~98 cal for the whole cake). Sub-recipe (1 batch).
a69655a4-d4fe-4e37-a6e5-4f73a4cf6987.author: user

steps.instructionText: Melt the baking chocolate gently (microwave in 15-sec bursts, or a double boiler). Whisk in the Splenda and the WARM almond milk a little at a time until glossy and pourable — adding it cold can seize the chocolate. Use it warm; it sets as it cools.

b1fb18a4-7de9-46ad-bf55-c7ca285122f0.name: Turtle Protein Cheesecake
b1fb18a4-7de9-46ad-bf55-c7ca285122f0.author: user
b1fb18a4-7de9-46ad-bf55-c7ca285122f0.commitMessage: derive variant from Vanilla Pumpkin Protein Cheesecake

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks, and we want a dense set anyway).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

payload.instructionText: Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.

a9d0698d-4a48-49d0-aa41-9e6af2d6673d.description: Turtle protein cheesecake derived from the Vanilla Pumpkin base — pecan skipped, so caramel + chocolate carry the turtle identity. Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11.5 cal/g protein (~217 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.

6b4d07bd-948e-49c2-b813-56ec256cbbe9.name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies (Oat, Low-Butter)
6b4d07bd-948e-49c2-b813-56ec256cbbe9.author: user
6b4d07bd-948e-49c2-b813-56ec256cbbe9.commitMessage: fork: oat + low-butter choc-chip test variant

steps.instructionText: Brown the butter: melt it in a pan over medium heat, swirling, until golden and nutty-smelling (3-4 min). Pour into a mixing bowl and let cool a couple minutes so it won't cook the egg.
Mix wet: whisk in the brown sugar, Splenda, whole egg, egg white, and vanilla until smooth and glossy. Don't whip air in - just combine.

Add dry: stir in the whey, flour, pudding mix, baking soda, and salt until a thick, scoopable dough forms, then fold in the chocolate chips. It should hold its shape, not pour. Too stiff/dry -> a splash of milk; too loose -> a bit more flour. Taste for sweetness.
Chill the dough 30 minutes.
Shape into 3 tall mounds - do NOT flatten.
Bake at 350F / 175C for 9-11 minutes. Pull when the edges are set but the center still looks slightly underdone/soft. Stays pale - don't wait for golden tops.
Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.
Brown the butter: melt it in a pan over medium heat, swirling, until golden and nutty-smelling (3-4 min). Pour into a mixing bowl and let cool a couple minutes so it won't cook the egg.
Mix wet: whisk in the brown sugar, Splenda, whole egg, egg white, and vanilla until smooth and glossy. Don't whip air in - just combine.

Add dry: stir in the whey, flour, pudding mix, baking soda, and salt until a thick, scoopable dough forms, then fold in the chocolate chips. It should hold its shape, not pour. Too stiff/dry -> a splash of milk; too loose -> a bit more flour. Taste for sweetness.
Chill the dough 30 minutes.
Shape into 3 tall mounds - do NOT flatten.
Bake at 350F / 175C for 9-11 minutes. Pull when the edges are set but the center still looks slightly underdone/soft. Stays pale - don't wait for golden tops.
Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.

ccf23f19-cd42-42c9-aa40-83493187d16f.description: Lower-butter, oat-boosted spin on the browned-butter choc-chip cookie: butter cut to 8 g, AP flour down to 20 g with 15 g rolled oats folded in, and a splash of almond milk to bring the dough together. To make / test.

dde1b38e-043d-442a-a99c-1758fa37de6a.name: Vanilla Protein Cheesecake
dde1b38e-043d-442a-a99c-1758fa37de6a.description: Base protein cheesecake (vanilla SF pudding, pumpkin for moisture/body). Launch point for flavor variants. Low-fat / high-protein; pumpkin does the work fat does in a standard cheesecake. Tuned DENSE (Tier 1+2): pumpkin reduced 120->75g + 2 tbsp AP flour for a firmer set (AP because that's what's on hand — its gluten sets denser than cornstarch), baked DRY (no water bath) to a just-set center and chilled overnight — denser at near-zero macro cost (~129 cal/slice, ratio 9.1). Stays lean; if a bake comes out rubbery rather than densely creamy, add a little fat (more cream cheese or an extra yolk).
dde1b38e-043d-442a-a99c-1758fa37de6a.author: user
dde1b38e-043d-442a-a99c-1758fa37de6a.commitMessage: rename: drop Pumpkin from name (puree is functional moisture/body, not a flavor); recipe otherwise unchanged

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks, and we want a dense set anyway).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

payload.instructionText: Toast 65 g shredded coconut in a dry skillet over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until golden and fragrant (it colors fast then burns - watch it). Cool. Grind 45 g to a fine meal (spice grinder or food processor) and beat it into the filling with the wet ingredients in step 3 so the coconut disperses throughout; reserve the other 20 g for the topping.

ccffdad4-6a73-4dd1-a2aa-4a43e8d7655a.description: Toasted-coconut variant of the vanilla base. Coconut runs throughout the filling (45 g toasted, fine-ground, beaten in) plus a 20 g toasted-coconut topping; crustless to stay lean-ish. Coconut is the only flavor source (the oil is neutral) so it is toasted hard for aroma. Macro note: coconut is ~64% fat, so this runs richer than the base - about 183 cal/slice, ratio ~12.3 vs the base 129/9.1. To-make; a tsp of coconut extract (0 cal) is the planned next lever to deepen flavor and drop the shredded amount.

a3c4da69-7c69-43b0-8684-31563120b5f9.name: Crème Brûlée Protein Cheesecake
a3c4da69-7c69-43b0-8684-31563120b5f9.author: user
a3c4da69-7c69-43b0-8684-31563120b5f9.commitMessage: fork creme brulee variant off the vanilla base

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks, and we want a dense set anyway).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

a94c11c4-71db-44af-99bf-ae088862e5a5.name: Crème Brûlée Protein Cheesecake
a94c11c4-71db-44af-99bf-ae088862e5a5.author: user
a94c11c4-71db-44af-99bf-ae088862e5a5.commitMessage: add brulee finishing step (broiler + dry-pan shard methods)

payload.instructionText: Brulee the top just before serving (the shell stays crisp only ~30 min on the cake). Dust about 1.5 tbsp (20 g) brown sugar over the cold cheesecake in a thin, even layer. BROILER METHOD: rack at the top, broiler on high; freeze the cake 15-20 min first so the sugar caramelizes before the filling warms, then broil 1-4 min, rotating, watching every second - pull the moment it is an even bubbling amber, then let it set ~1 min into a crackable glass. DRY-PAN SHARD METHOD (no broiler): melt the 20 g brown sugar in a dry nonstick pan over medium heat, swirling (do not stir) until liquid and amber, pour thin onto parchment, cool until hard, snap into shards, and stand them on top just before serving.

c742e176-1409-43be-b35e-1857f8dea460.description: Creme brulee cheesecake: the vanilla base chilled, finished with a caramelized brown-sugar shell. A cheesecake is a baked custard, so the brulee crust is all it needs - and the shell uses so little sugar it is nearly free (~+10 cal/slice). Vanilla bumped to 2 tsp for a custard-forward nose. The crust needs REAL sugar (sucralose will not caramelize) - brown sugar gives a deep molasses caramel and is the one place Splenda is skipped. Two finishes in the method: oven broiler (freeze first) or dry-pan caramel shards (no torch needed). About 140 cal/slice, ratio ~9.8 - the lean, high-drama variant. To-make.

86efdcbe-447a-419f-94a0-56f361714e77.name: Protein Butterscotch Yogurt Pockets / Swirl
86efdcbe-447a-419f-94a0-56f361714e77.description: Protein caramel for marbling into the turtle cheesecake filling — 0% Greek yogurt + butterscotch SF pudding (the caramel note) + granulated Splenda, salted. Thick and spoonable; it lives hidden in the swirl, so it carries protein, not gloss. Sub-recipe (1 batch ~= 99 cal / 12.6 P).
86efdcbe-447a-419f-94a0-56f361714e77.author: user
86efdcbe-447a-419f-94a0-56f361714e77.commitMessage: rename: drop Turtle (sub-recipes get general names); it is a butterscotch yogurt swirl, not real caramel

steps.instructionText: Whisk the Greek yogurt, butterscotch pudding mix, Splenda, almond milk, and salt smooth. It should be thick and spoonable like a loose caramel — chill 10 min if you want it firmer to swirl.

c96ac927-b3c8-45a8-b496-87fa420e8569.name: Brown-Sugar Caramel
c96ac927-b3c8-45a8-b496-87fa420e8569.description: Real glossy caramel - brown sugar cooked with a little butter, thinned with almond milk, salted. Genuine caramelized-sugar flavor (you cannot caramelize Splenda), kept small. Reusable across anything that needs caramel - cook it to the texture you want: saucy, thick, or caramel-apple-thick (see the finishing step). Sub-recipe, 1 batch, ~121 cal.
c96ac927-b3c8-45a8-b496-87fa420e8569.author: user
c96ac927-b3c8-45a8-b496-87fa420e8569.commitMessage: rename: drop Turtle - just caramel, reusable anywhere

steps.instructionText: Melt the butter in a small saucepan, add the brown sugar and salt, and cook over medium, stirring, until it bubbles and darkens slightly (~2-3 min). Off heat, whisk in the almond milk (it will sputter) until smooth. Cool slightly — it thickens as it sits; rewarm to drizzle.

8ac72ed5-4b00-44b4-b3e3-2f3ee0a16bd1.name: Brown-Sugar Caramel
8ac72ed5-4b00-44b4-b3e3-2f3ee0a16bd1.description: Real glossy caramel - brown sugar cooked with a little butter, thinned with almond milk, salted. Genuine caramelized-sugar flavor (you cannot caramelize Splenda), kept small. Reusable across anything that needs caramel - cook it to the texture you want: saucy, thick, or caramel-apple-thick (see the finishing step). Sub-recipe, 1 batch, ~121 cal.
8ac72ed5-4b00-44b4-b3e3-2f3ee0a16bd1.author: user

steps.instructionText: Melt the butter in a small saucepan, add the brown sugar and salt, and cook over medium, stirring, until it bubbles and darkens slightly (~2-3 min). Off heat, whisk in the almond milk (it will sputter) until smooth. Cool slightly — it thickens as it sits; rewarm to drizzle.

FINISH TO YOUR TEXTURE (reusable - take it only as far as you need): SAUCY/pourable - stop right after the almond milk is whisked smooth; it coats a spoon thinly and pours in a thread, for drizzle (~225F). THICK/spoonable - keep on low and reduce 1-2 min more until it mounds and falls in a slow ribbon; sets like dulce de leche and holds a swirl (~235-240F, soft-ball). CARAMEL-APPLE THICK/chewy - reduce further until a drop in cold water forms a soft pliable ball (~245-250F, firm-ball); off heat it clings and sets tacky-chewy, for dipping or a caramel that stays put. No thermometer? Cold-water drop test - thin thread = saucy, soft ball = spoonable, firmer ball = chewy.

0c0b1b5f-c6d5-4766-b9d8-2735735bc2f4.name: Turtle Protein Cheesecake
0c0b1b5f-c6d5-4766-b9d8-2735735bc2f4.author: user
0c0b1b5f-c6d5-4766-b9d8-2735735bc2f4.commitMessage: re-point swirl pin to the renamed sub-recipe

payload.instructionText: Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.

0c0b1b5f-c6d5-4766-b9d8-2735735bc2f4.description: Turtle protein cheesecake derived from the Vanilla Pumpkin base — pecan skipped, so caramel + chocolate carry the turtle identity. Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11.5 cal/g protein (~217 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.

02a3283a-eda6-4eeb-b01f-921cf3d653bc.commitMessage: bump brulee shell 20 -> 30 g brown sugar (more pronounced crackle)

payload.instructionText: Brulee the top just before serving (the shell stays crisp only ~30 min on the cake). Dust about 1.5 tbsp (20 g) brown sugar over the cold cheesecake in a thin, even layer. BROILER METHOD: rack at the top, broiler on high; freeze the cake 15-20 min first so the sugar caramelizes before the filling warms, then broil 1-4 min, rotating, watching every second - pull the moment it is an even bubbling amber, then let it set ~1 min into a crackable glass. DRY-PAN SHARD METHOD (no broiler): melt the 20 g brown sugar in a dry nonstick pan over medium heat, swirling (do not stir) until liquid and amber, pour thin onto parchment, cool until hard, snap into shards, and stand them on top just before serving.

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks, and we want a dense set anyway).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

Brulee the top just before serving (the shell stays crisp only ~30 min on the cake). Dust about 1.5 tbsp (20 g) brown sugar over the cold cheesecake in a thin, even layer. BROILER METHOD: rack at the top, broiler on high; freeze the cake 15-20 min first so the sugar caramelizes before the filling warms, then broil 1-4 min, rotating, watching every second - pull the moment it is an even bubbling amber, then let it set ~1 min into a crackable glass. DRY-PAN SHARD METHOD (no broiler): melt the 20 g brown sugar in a dry nonstick pan over medium heat, swirling (do not stir) until liquid and amber, pour thin onto parchment, cool until hard, snap into shards, and stand them on top just before serving.

02a3283a-eda6-4eeb-b01f-921cf3d653bc.description: Creme brulee cheesecake: the vanilla base chilled, finished with a caramelized brown-sugar shell. A cheesecake is a baked custard, so the brulee crust is all it needs - and the shell uses so little sugar it is nearly free (~+10 cal/slice). Vanilla bumped to 2 tsp for a custard-forward nose. The crust needs REAL sugar (sucralose will not caramelize) - brown sugar gives a deep molasses caramel and is the one place Splenda is skipped. Two finishes in the method: oven broiler (freeze first) or dry-pan caramel shards (no torch needed). About 140 cal/slice, ratio ~9.8 - the lean, high-drama variant. To-make.

a730558c-2789-4236-8ff7-c244e91455ba.description: Creme brulee cheesecake on a graham crust: vanilla custard cheesecake over a par-baked graham crust, finished with a torched brown-sugar shell. A cheesecake is a baked custard, so the brulee crust is the signature; the graham base adds the classic restaurant form. The shell needs REAL sugar (sucralose will not caramelize) - ~30 g brown sugar in a thin even layer; everything else stays Splenda. Two finishes in the method: oven broiler (freeze first) or dry-pan caramel shards (no torch). About 183 cal/slice with the crust + bigger shell (was 140 crustless). To-make.

1db15df3-a61a-4a81-968f-37e8a55d1f2c.name: Dark-Chocolate Ganache
1db15df3-a61a-4a81-968f-37e8a55d1f2c.description: Chocolate drizzle for the top — unsweetened baking chocolate melted with granulated Splenda and thinned with warm almond milk. Real cocoa-butter mouthfeel that cocoa powder can't fake; kept to a thin drizzle so the fat stays small (~98 cal for the whole cake). Sub-recipe (1 batch).
1db15df3-a61a-4a81-968f-37e8a55d1f2c.author: user
1db15df3-a61a-4a81-968f-37e8a55d1f2c.commitMessage: rename: drop Turtle (sub-recipes get general names)

steps.instructionText: Melt the baking chocolate gently (microwave in 15-sec bursts, or a double boiler). Whisk in the Splenda and the WARM almond milk a little at a time until glossy and pourable — adding it cold can seize the chocolate. Use it warm; it sets as it cools.

da33ba22-0cb6-4868-ab15-b3a6ce34f3f7.name: Chocolate Graham Crust
da33ba22-0cb6-4868-ab15-b3a6ce34f3f7.author: user
da33ba22-0cb6-4868-ab15-b3a6ce34f3f7.commitMessage: rename: drop Turtle (sub-recipes get general names)

payload.instructionText: Stir the graham crumbs, cocoa, Splenda, and salt together. Work in the melted butter and egg white until it clumps like wet sand, then press firmly into the bottom of a parchment-lined 9-inch pan — run the parchment up two opposite sides as a sling so you can lift the whole cake out later. Bake at 350F for ~10 min, then cool while you finish the filling.

da33ba22-0cb6-4868-ab15-b3a6ce34f3f7.description: The Turtle's crust — the base Protein Graham Crust forked to add unsweetened cocoa for a chocolate, graham-forward bottom. Same egg-white binding and par-bake; cocoa is the only addition. Sub-recipe (1 batch ~= 319 cal).

a2f8d2bc-c07a-41bc-8d16-4db222135e1b.name: Turtle Protein Cheesecake
a2f8d2bc-c07a-41bc-8d16-4db222135e1b.author: user
a2f8d2bc-c07a-41bc-8d16-4db222135e1b.commitMessage: re-point ganache pin to renamed sub-recipe

payload.instructionText: Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.

a2f8d2bc-c07a-41bc-8d16-4db222135e1b.description: Turtle protein cheesecake derived from the Vanilla Pumpkin base — pecan skipped, so caramel + chocolate carry the turtle identity. Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11.5 cal/g protein (~217 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.

6dfc19fa-79f5-452e-947b-22a0f3782987.name: Turtle Protein Cheesecake
6dfc19fa-79f5-452e-947b-22a0f3782987.author: user
6dfc19fa-79f5-452e-947b-22a0f3782987.commitMessage: re-point chocolate-graham-crust pin to renamed sub-recipe

payload.instructionText: Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks, and we want a dense set anyway).

Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325F for ~45 min (or 350F for ~35-40 min if you want it faster) — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.

Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.

Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11.5 cal/g protein (~217 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.

71b51316-34fe-4f81-84f5-308d788e21ec.name: Crème Brûlée Protein Cheesecake
71b51316-34fe-4f81-84f5-308d788e21ec.author: user
71b51316-34fe-4f81-84f5-308d788e21ec.commitMessage: fix(brulee): shell prose now says 30 g (2 tbsp), matching the 30 g usage (was stale 20 g)

payload.instructionText: Brulee the top just before serving (the shell stays crisp only ~30 min on the cake). Dust about 1.5 tbsp (20 g) brown sugar over the cold cheesecake in a thin, even layer. BROILER METHOD: rack at the top, broiler on high; freeze the cake 15-20 min first so the sugar caramelizes before the filling warms, then broil 1-4 min, rotating, watching every second - pull the moment it is an even bubbling amber, then let it set ~1 min into a crackable glass. DRY-PAN SHARD METHOD (no broiler): melt the 20 g brown sugar in a dry nonstick pan over medium heat, swirling (do not stir) until liquid and amber, pour thin onto parchment, cool until hard, snap into shards, and stand them on top just before serving.
Make and par-bake the graham crust (see the composed crust steps below), then cool it in the pan while you make the filling. The brulee filling gets poured over this crust.
Pour the filling over the cooled par-baked graham crust. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Brulee the top just before serving (the shell stays crisp only ~30 min on the cake). Dust about 2 tbsp (30 g) brown sugar over the cold cheesecake in a thin, even layer. BROILER METHOD: rack at the top, broiler on high; freeze the cake 15-20 min first so the sugar caramelizes before the filling warms, then broil 1-4 min, rotating, watching every second - pull the moment it is an even bubbling amber, then let it set ~1 min into a crackable glass. DRY-PAN SHARD METHOD (no broiler): melt the 30 g brown sugar in a dry nonstick pan over medium heat, swirling (do not stir) until liquid and amber, pour thin onto parchment, cool until hard, snap into shards, and stand them on top just before serving.

71b51316-34fe-4f81-84f5-308d788e21ec.description: Creme brulee cheesecake on a graham crust: vanilla custard cheesecake over a par-baked graham crust, finished with a torched brown-sugar shell. A cheesecake is a baked custard, so the brulee crust is the signature; the graham base adds the classic restaurant form. The shell needs REAL sugar (sucralose will not caramelize) - ~30 g brown sugar in a thin even layer; everything else stays Splenda. Two finishes in the method: oven broiler (freeze first) or dry-pan caramel shards (no torch). About 183 cal/slice with the crust + bigger shell (was 140 crustless). To-make.

b7bd4fb0-9f77-4f68-a0ab-444abce9cd3d.name: High-Protein Sugar Cookies
b7bd4fb0-9f77-4f68-a0ab-444abce9cd3d.description: Low-flour protein sugar cookie tuned to kill the rubbery 'pancake' texture: a scoop of whey replaces most flour and SF instant pudding binds moisture for a dense bakery chew. Sweetener is granulated Splenda; pudding modeled as the Simply Delish SF vanilla already in the library; butter is regular unsalted, already halved to 7 g. Leavening is baking powder only (no soda / cream of tartar). Final step is a fridge rest. Source: user. Stated batch macros ~428 kcal / 44P / 41C / 11F (107/cookie at 4).
b7bd4fb0-9f77-4f68-a0ab-444abce9cd3d.author: user
b7bd4fb0-9f77-4f68-a0ab-444abce9cd3d.commitMessage: rename: drop 'Anti-Pancake' from the title (technique still described in the body)

steps.instructionText: Whisk the dry base — AP flour, whey protein, SF pudding mix, Splenda, baking powder, salt — until there are no protein clumps.
Add the melted butter, whole egg, Greek yogurt, and vanilla into the dry mix.
Stir to combine, then stop and let the bowl sit untouched 5 minutes so the whey and pudding starches swell into a shapeable dough. If still tacky, chill 5 more minutes.
Preheat oven/air fryer to 325F. Divide into 4 equal balls on parchment and press down firmly with your palm or a glass — this dough sets in the shape you give it, it won't spread.
Bake at 325F for 6-7 minutes. Pull the moment the edges look dry and set, even if the centers still look slightly soft — whey dries out fast.
Leave the cookies on the hot sheet 5-10 minutes; residual pan heat finishes the centers without making the protein rubbery.
Final step: rest in the fridge ~10 minutes to fully set, then eat.
Preheat to 325°F (165°C) and line or grease a small ~6-inch square pan.
Melt the butter + baking chocolate over low heat or in short microwave bursts. Stir in the cocoa + espresso powder to bloom them in the warm fat — it becomes a thick paste.

Stir in the skim milk to loosen and finish blooming; warm gently to combine. For chunky brownies, reserve 1–2 tsp of the thick paste, stir a pinch of Splenda into it, and set aside. Off the heat, cool to just warm.
Stir in the Splenda, then the Greek yogurt and vanilla.
Lightly beat the egg (no whipping — keep air out so it bakes fudgy, not cakey) and stir in last. If the base is still warm, temper first: stir a spoonful of base into the egg, then add it back.

Whisk the flour + cornstarch + protein powder + salt, then fold into the batter by hand just until no dry streaks remain — then stop. A few small lumps are fine; mixing past this develops gluten and bakes up cakey. Smooth finish: fold uniform. Chunky finish: fold in the reserved sweetened paste at the very end so it stays in pockets. Spread in the pan.
Bake at 325°F for ~18 minutes, checking from ~14. Pull at a slight center jiggle (not a liquid slosh) — it should look a touch underdone; carryover heat and cooling finish the set.

Doneness: edges set and just pulling from the sides with a slight wobble in the center (not a liquid slosh). A toothpick should read moist crumbs clinging — a clean toothpick means overbaked. On these small protein batters, trust the jiggle over the toothpick.
Cool completely in the pan on a wire rack, undisturbed — never cut warm. The crumb sets and the protein firms as it cools; a warm one seems gummy and then comes good.

Chill a few hours or overnight — they're better the next day. Cut cold with a clean knife (for the fudgy ones, run it under hot water and wipe between cuts). Store airtight; freeze portions for longer keeping. Optional: microwave a piece 8–10 sec to soften the fat — no longer, or the protein turns rubbery.

83c92af6-1d7c-48bb-b643-4c66ae2e1024.name: Fudgy + Chewy Protein Brownies
83c92af6-1d7c-48bb-b643-4c66ae2e1024.author: user

Cool completely in the pan on a wire rack, undisturbed — never cut warm. The crumb sets and the protein firms as it cools; a warm one seems gummy and then comes good.

Chill a few hours or overnight — they're better the next day. Cut cold with a clean knife (for the fudgy ones, run it under hot water and wipe between cuts). Store airtight; freeze portions for longer keeping. Optional: microwave a piece 8–10 sec to soften the fat — no longer, or the protein turns rubbery.

d9829c01-1a07-42dc-b62e-5e36f27ca734.name: Chewiest Protein Brownies
d9829c01-1a07-42dc-b62e-5e36f27ca734.author: user
d9829c01-1a07-42dc-b62e-5e36f27ca734.commitMessage: derive variant from Fudgy Protein Brownies

steps.instructionText: Preheat to 325°F (165°C) and line or grease a small ~6-inch square pan.
Melt the butter + baking chocolate over low heat or in short microwave bursts. Stir in the cocoa + espresso powder to bloom them in the warm fat — it becomes a thick paste.

Stir in the skim milk to loosen and finish blooming; warm gently to combine. For chunky brownies, reserve 1–2 tsp of the thick paste, stir a pinch of Splenda into it, and set aside. Off the heat, cool to just warm.
Stir in the Splenda, then the Greek yogurt and vanilla.
Lightly beat the egg (no whipping — keep air out so it bakes fudgy, not cakey) and stir in last. If the base is still warm, temper first: stir a spoonful of base into the egg, then add it back.

Whisk the flour + cornstarch + protein powder + salt, then fold into the batter by hand just until no dry streaks remain — then stop. A few small lumps are fine; mixing past this develops gluten and bakes up cakey. Smooth finish: fold uniform. Chunky finish: fold in the reserved sweetened paste at the very end so it stays in pockets. Spread in the pan.
Bake at 325°F for ~18 minutes, checking from ~14. Pull at a slight center jiggle (not a liquid slosh) — it should look a touch underdone; carryover heat and cooling finish the set.

Doneness: edges set and just pulling from the sides with a slight wobble in the center (not a liquid slosh). A toothpick should read moist crumbs clinging — a clean toothpick means overbaked. On these small protein batters, trust the jiggle over the toothpick.
Cool completely in the pan on a wire rack, undisturbed — never cut warm. The crumb sets and the protein firms as it cools; a warm one seems gummy and then comes good.

Chill a few hours or overnight — they're better the next day. Cut cold with a clean knife (for the fudgy ones, run it under hot water and wipe between cuts). Store airtight; freeze portions for longer keeping. Optional: microwave a piece 8–10 sec to soften the fat — no longer, or the protein turns rubbery.

Whisk the flour + cornstarch + protein powder + salt, then fold into the batter by hand just until no dry streaks remain — then stop. A few small lumps are fine; mixing past this develops gluten and bakes up cakey. Smooth finish: fold uniform. Chunky finish: fold in the reserved sweetened paste at the very end so it stays in pockets. Spread in the pan.
Bake at 325°F for ~18 minutes, checking from ~14. Pull at a slight center jiggle (not a liquid slosh) — it should look a touch underdone; carryover heat and cooling finish the set.

Doneness: edges set and just pulling from the sides with a slight wobble in the center (not a liquid slosh). A toothpick should read moist crumbs clinging — a clean toothpick means overbaked. On these small protein batters, trust the jiggle over the toothpick.
Cool completely in the pan on a wire rack, undisturbed — never cut warm. The crumb sets and the protein firms as it cools; a warm one seems gummy and then comes good.

Chill a few hours or overnight — they're better the next day. Cut cold with a clean knife (for the fudgy ones, run it under hot water and wipe between cuts). Store airtight; freeze portions for longer keeping. Optional: microwave a piece 8–10 sec to soften the fat — no longer, or the protein turns rubbery.

Whisk the flour + protein powder + salt, then fold in to no dry streaks, then ~10–15 extra strokes to develop a little gluten for chew — still stopping before it goes very stretchy or tough. No chocolate, no cornstarch.
Stir in the Splenda; add a splash more skim milk only if stiff. Spread in the pan.
Bake at 325°F for ~18 minutes, checking from ~14. Pull at only a faint center wobble — chewy wants a touch more set than fudgy, but don't dry it out.

Doneness: edges set and just pulling from the sides with a slight wobble in the center (not a liquid slosh). A toothpick should read moist crumbs clinging — a clean toothpick means overbaked. On these small protein batters, trust the jiggle over the toothpick.
Cool completely in the pan on a wire rack, undisturbed — never cut warm. The crumb sets and the protein firms as it cools; a warm one seems gummy and then comes good.

Chill a few hours or overnight — they're better the next day. Cut cold with a clean knife (for the fudgy ones, run it under hot water and wipe between cuts). Store airtight; freeze portions for longer keeping. Optional: microwave a piece 8–10 sec to soften the fat — no longer, or the protein turns rubbery.

Cool completely in the pan on a wire rack, undisturbed — never cut warm. The crumb sets and the protein firms as it cools; a warm one seems gummy and then comes good.

Chill a few hours or overnight — they're better the next day. Cut cold with a clean knife (for the fudgy ones, run it under hot water and wipe between cuts). Store airtight; freeze portions for longer keeping. Optional: microwave a piece 8–10 sec to soften the fat — no longer, or the protein turns rubbery.

a3345443-c46b-4c20-b17b-765f9611cd3b.name: Fudgy Protein Brownies
a3345443-c46b-4c20-b17b-765f9611cd3b.description: The fudge-maximized protein brownie — the original of a three-texture set, finished smooth (uniformly fudgy) or chunky (sweetened dark-chocolate pockets). Fudge comes from butter + a little unsweetened baking chocolate, with the egg barely beaten and stirred in last to keep air out (air bakes cakey). Cocoa + espresso are bloomed in the warm fat; sweetener is 1/4 cup granulated Splenda (never monk fruit). Bakes at 325F for ~18 min, pulled at a slight center jiggle, then cooled fully and chilled — better the next day. Computed ~629 cal / 38 g protein per batch of 9; ~70 cal, 4.3P, 7.2C, 3.4F per square; 16.5 cal per g protein. Source: user.
a3345443-c46b-4c20-b17b-765f9611cd3b.author: user

steps.instructionText: Preheat to 325°F (165°C) and line or grease a small ~6-inch square pan.
Melt the butter + baking chocolate over low heat or in short microwave bursts. Stir in the cocoa + espresso powder to bloom them in the warm fat — it becomes a thick paste.

Stir in the skim milk to loosen and finish blooming; warm gently to combine. For chunky brownies, reserve 1–2 tsp of the thick paste, stir a pinch of Splenda into it, and set aside. Off the heat, cool to just warm.
Stir in the Splenda, then the Greek yogurt and vanilla.
Lightly beat the egg (no whipping — keep air out so it bakes fudgy, not cakey) and stir in last. If the base is still warm, temper first: stir a spoonful of base into the egg, then add it back.

Whisk the flour + cornstarch + protein powder + salt, then fold into the batter by hand just until no dry streaks remain — then stop. A few small lumps are fine; mixing past this develops gluten and bakes up cakey. Smooth finish: fold uniform. Chunky finish: fold in the reserved sweetened paste at the very end so it stays in pockets. Spread in the pan.
Bake at 325°F for ~18 minutes, checking from ~14. Pull at a slight center jiggle (not a liquid slosh) — it should look a touch underdone; carryover heat and cooling finish the set.

Doneness: edges set and just pulling from the sides with a slight wobble in the center (not a liquid slosh). A toothpick should read moist crumbs clinging — a clean toothpick means overbaked. On these small protein batters, trust the jiggle over the toothpick.
Cool completely in the pan on a wire rack, undisturbed — never cut warm. The crumb sets and the protein firms as it cools; a warm one seems gummy and then comes good.

Chill a few hours or overnight — they're better the next day. Cut cold with a clean knife (for the fudgy ones, run it under hot water and wipe between cuts). Store airtight; freeze portions for longer keeping. Optional: microwave a piece 8–10 sec to soften the fat — no longer, or the protein turns rubbery.

99f0910c-ff50-49a8-a20b-88de89b2bdd4.author: user

payload.instructionText: Melt the butter + baking chocolate (low heat or short microwave bursts). Stir in the cocoa + espresso to bloom into a paste; set aside to cool slightly.

Beat the whole egg + egg white with the brown sugar only until the sugar grit dissolves and it looks smooth and glossy — then STOP. This builds chew and the shiny top; do NOT whip it to a pale, voluminous ribbon, which aerates it and bakes up holey/cakey.
Stir the Greek yogurt into the egg mixture, then fold in the cooled chocolate mixture until uniform.
Whisk the flour + protein powder + salt, then fold in just to no dry streaks — then a few extra gentle strokes for a little chew, stopping before the batter turns smooth and stretchy. No cornstarch.
Stir in the Splenda; add a splash of skim milk only if the batter is stiff. Spread in the pan.
Bake at 325°F for ~17–18 minutes, checking from ~14. Pull at a slight center jiggle — it's fudgy-leaning, so on the early side.

99f0910c-ff50-49a8-a20b-88de89b2bdd4.description: The balanced one — derived from the Fudgy base, keeping real fudge from butter + baking chocolate but adding chew: the whole egg + an extra egg white are beaten with brown sugar until glossy (dissolved, never whipped to a ribbon), the cornstarch is dropped, and protein is doubled. 1/4 cup granulated Splenda, no added milk, baked at 325F for ~17-18 min and pulled at a slight (fudgy-leaning) jiggle. Doubling protein at flat calories pulls the ratio from the base's 16.5 down to 12.5 cal per g protein. Computed ~624 cal / 50 g protein per batch of 9; ~69 cal, 5.6P, 6.7C, 2.8F per square. Source: user.
